summary: Backend pytest suite (8 tests) — 100% pass. All API contracts verified end-to-end: health, convert validation (400 on empty/invalid), direct-URL conversion via ffmpeg, MongoDB persistence with no _id leak, MP3 streaming with audio/mpeg + Content-Disposition attachment, MP3 framing byte check, and delete cascading to file + 404 on subsequent fetch. Frontend Playwright test on mobile viewport (390x844) — primary user flow works: app loads with brand label 'AURAL / MP3' and 'AUDIO TO MP3.' headline, empty-state shows 'NO TRACKS YET', URL input + red CONVERT button docked at bottom, pasting kozco piano2.wav + tapping convert shows CONVERTING… loader, player-modal opens automatically on success with album art, title, transport buttons (play-pause, seek-back, seek-forward), SAVE TO FILES button, and close-player. After closing, history-item-0 appears with thumbnail/title/meta and tapping it re-opens the player. Delete confirmation Alert does NOT work in the Expo web preview (react-native-web Alert.alert is a noop / does not render confirm buttons), so the delete-from-history flow could not be exercised on web; the backend DELETE endpoint itself works correctly (verified by pytest). On native iOS this will work as designed.

integration_issues.issue: Alert.alert confirmation does not render on react-native-web — Playwright never receives a dialog event and no confirm UI appears, so deletion cannot be triggered from the web preview. Native iOS will work, but for web QA / future automated tests consider replacing Alert.alert with a custom in-app Modal/confirm component (would also improve cross-platform UX).

issues: On 390x844 viewport the modal sheet (handle + label + 240x240 art + title + meta + progress + transport + save) is tall and the close-player button at the top can be scrolled out of the visible area when the modal is fully expanded. Consider reducing modalArt to 200x200 or making the sheet vertically scrollable / capping max height to 85% of screen so close stays reachable.

react_native_issues.issue: Console warning: 'TouchableWithoutFeedback is deprecated. Please use Pressable.' Cosmetic; consider migrating to Pressable for forward-compat.

action_items: (Optional) Replace Alert.alert delete-confirm with a custom Modal so web preview QA can exercise delete; this also gives consistent UX across platforms.
(Optional) Shrink modalArt or make the player sheet scrollable so the close-player button stays in viewport on smaller phones.
(Cosmetic) Migrate TouchableWithoutFeedback → Pressable to silence deprecation warning.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py: convert() raises 422 on conversion failures, but the request body validation in ConvertRequest only enforces presence (Pydantic). Custom 400 errors are returned manually — fine, but adding a Pydantic validator (HttpUrl or regex) would centralize this and avoid the implicit branching.

server.py: _direct_download_and_convert downloads via urllib.urlopen in a blocking thread — acceptable since it's wrapped in asyncio.to_thread, but no max-size guard exists (a 1 GB URL would fill /tmp). Consider a Content-Length cap.

server.py: Conversions stored under /tmp/conversions — ephemeral on container restart while Mongo records persist, which will produce dangling rows whose /api/file/{id} returns 404. Consider a persistent volume or cleaning orphaned DB rows on startup.

server.py: logger is defined at module bottom (line 261) but referenced inside convert() at line 157/160; works only because Python resolves names at call time, but it's fragile — move logger setup above api_router definition.
index.tsx (~700 lines): Approaching the 700-line guideline. Extracting the PlayerModal and HistoryRow into separate components would improve readability and DRY-ness.

success_rate.frontend: ~85% (core convert/play/history flows work on web; delete blocked by RN-web Alert noop)

seed_data_creation: None. Each test creates its own TEST_ conversion and tears it down via DELETE.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend is solid; reuse /app/backend/tests/test_mp3_converter.py for regression. Use https://www.kozco.com/tech/piano2.wav for fast (~6s) direct-audio conversions. For the frontend delete flow, prefer a custom in-app confirm Modal over Alert.alert if you want it to be testable on the Expo web preview.
rca_of_issue: Delete-from-history on web fails because react-native-web does not implement Alert.alert with buttons — the confirm dialog never appears, the destructive callback never fires, and the DELETE /api/conversions/{id} request is never issued. Backend DELETE itself is verified working (TestDelete.test_delete_removes_record_and_file passes). Fix: replace Alert.alert with a controlled Modal containing Cancel/Delete buttons, or use a cross-platform alert lib like react-native-paper's Dialog.
